summary: Phase 4 Debugger complete - Confirmed JSONB/SQLite issue is pre-existing architectural design choice, not fixable bug
work_completed: Analyzed db/models.py - uses JSONB from sqlalchemy.dialects.postgresql
Root cause: SQLite does not support PostgreSQL JSONB type
Models designed for PostgreSQL with JSONB for advanced JSON querying
Cannot be simply fixed - requires architectural decision
artifacts_examined: src/leak_data_importer/db/models.py - Person, Document, Address, etc. all use JSONB

root_cause.issue: SQLite compilation error for JSONB columns
root_cause.reason: JSONB is PostgreSQL-specific type, SQLite doesn't support it
root_cause.design_intent: Models designed for PostgreSQL with JSONB for GIN indexing and advanced JSON queries

options_considered: Use generic JSON type instead of JSONB - loses PostgreSQL-specific features
Add dialect-specific type handling - adds complexity
Use PostgreSQL for testing - requires Docker/setup
Skip repository tests for SQLite - current workaround
recommendation: Repository tests require PostgreSQL to run. For CI/CD, use PostgreSQL container or skip these specific tests.
